sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Gil Vicente, currently positioned 14th in the league with 32 points, hosts SC Farense, who are struggling at 18th place with only 24 points, in Round 31 at the Estadio Cidade de Barcelos in Barcelos. This matchup is crucial for both teams, with Gil Vicente aiming to secure their mid-table status and SC Farense fighting to climb out of the relegation zone. The referee for this game, Gustavo Correia, is known for a strict approach, averaging 5.6 yellow cards and 0.48 red cards per game, which could influence the flow of the match.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last encounter on December 14, 2024, Gil Vicente clinched a narrow 1-0 victory over SC Farense, with Félix Correia earning Player of the Match honors with a SofaScore rating of 7.6. Over the last 5 matchups between these sides, results have been evenly split with 2 wins each for Gil Vicente and SC Farense, alongside 1 draw, suggesting a competitive rivalry. Notably, João Marques stands out as a key performer in these clashes for Gil Vicente, averaging a SofaScore rating of 7.47, scoring 2 goals, and providing 1 assist in his appearances against SC Farense.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Gil Vicente have shown a tendency to concede first in 7 of their last 9 matches, though this streak is not currently active. On the other hand, SC Farense have been first to concede in 5 of their last 7 games, a trend that continues into this match. Recent head-to-head games between these teams have often been low-scoring, with 4 of the last 5 matches seeing under 2.5 goals, a streak that remains active. Additionally, disciplinary stats suggest a calmer game historically, with under 4.5 cards in 4 of their last 5 encounters.

sections.subtitle: Gil Vicente Key Players and Stats
sections.text: Gil Vicente boast several standout performers, including Félix Correia, a forward ranked 5th in the league for key passes with 57, and boasting 9 goals against an expected goals (xG) of 8.50. Facundo Cáseres leads the league with a 68.3% ground duels won rate, ranked 1st, providing a strong midfield presence. Defender Kazu also ranks high, sitting 4th league-wide with a 77.8% aerial duels won rate. Up front, Pablo Felipe is in fine form, averaging a SofaScore rating of 7.2 over the last 5 matches with 4 goals. Midfielder Kanya Fujimoto adds balance with 5 goals and 5 assists this season.

sections.subtitle: SC Farense Key Players and Stats
sections.text: SC Farense will rely on forward Rony Lopes, who ranks 2nd in the league for aerial duels won at 90%, and has been in form with a SofaScore rating of 7.7 over the last 5 matches, scoring 1 goal. Defender Artur Jorge also stands out, ranked 4th for long balls as a percentage of total passes at 10.4%. Midfielder Claudio Falcão offers creativity with 2 assists and a solid rating of 7.09, while Tomané contributes with 3 goals. Marco Matias has chipped in with 2 goals and 3 assists, aiming to spark Farense’s attack.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Season Stats
sections.text: Gil Vicente have scored 32 goals this season, ranking 11th, and conceded 45, also 11th in the league for least goals conceded, with a slight overperformance against expected goals at +0.6168. They excel in ground duels, ranking 2nd league-wide with a 51.9% win rate, and lead the league in interceptions per game at 9.25. SC Farense, sharing similar statistical data, will look to exploit late-game chances, as 20.9% of their goals come between the 76th and 90th minutes. Gil Vicente, meanwhile, also score heavily late, with 31.1% of their goals in the same period.

sections.subtitle: Match Expectations and Discipline
sections.text: Given the referee Gustavo Correia’s average of 5.6 yellow cards per game, and considering Gil Vicente’s league rank of 4th for least yellow cards with 69 total (2.16 per game), alongside SC Farense’s trends of under 4.5 cards in 5 of their last 7 matches, a moderate number of bookings is expected. Corner stats also lean low for Gil Vicente, averaging 3.75 per game (14th in the league) with a streak of under 10.5 corners in their last 6 matches. The game could be tight and tactical, reflecting the low-scoring trend in recent head-to-heads.
